scene.title: Boiler service timing
scene.summary: A boiler-service timing decision; nobody visits the utility cupboard and the outcome is purely private comfort.

mcq_options.text: Let the single closest peer set the tone on top of the private floor; the rest of the circle fades into background — around $346.
Ignore the peers altogether and commit to exactly the level that the private cost-and-need calculation would justify — about $341.
Combine the baseline commitment with a closeness-weighted blend of what the peers do, so familiar voices count more than distant ones — ends up near $344.
Anchor on the middle peer of the group and scale up the private floor by that median pull — arrives near $343.

cot_text: Reasoning: Scene-type flag: non-positional. The observable cues around the protagonist place this inside the non-positional category. Counterfactual check: this is not positional-consumption because no audience will see this commitment; there are no feeds or group chats that surface the choice; it is also not positional-human-capital because there is no cohort channel or leaderboard comparing the hours or the output. Without a visible audience there is no peer pull to layer on anything. The private baseline on this margin -- what the protagonist would commit to with nobody watching -- already captures the full reasonable level. Gold rule: hold the private floor; the target maintenance cost equals the baseline, near $344.
